title: Senior Engineer, Dry Process
description:
Job descrpition

  • Responsible for transfer and industrialisation of new products, processes and materials
  • Manages improvements in dry process with a widened interest to achieve organizational goals
  • Oversee process setup, qualification, and industrial performance stabilisation
  • Contribute to project planning, industrial readiness, and concept selection
  • Support concept selection and provide manufacturing input during the early design phase
  • Define and maintain industrialisation standards and best practices
  • Develop and manage technical capabilities and resource planning
  • Lead strategic manufacturing improvement projects and problem-solving task forces
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless implementation

Job requirements

  • Degree in Chemical / Material Engineering or equivalent
  • Strong experience in manufacturing industrialisation or process engineering
  • Strong communication, leadership, and problem-solving capabilities
  • Familiar with FMEA, SPC or other process improvement tools, as well as 8D and QRQC methodology
  • Good knowledge of Project Management methodology

postedCompany.description:
About Linxens

Linxens is a pioneer and a global leader in the design and manufacture of smartcard connectors, inlays and led light sources based on highly automated processes resulting in most competitive assembly costs for its customers. More than 30 years’ experience provides us the ability to produce large volumes of products that demonstrate exceptional standards of quality, reliability and costs.

Linxens offers innovative contact and contactless connectivity solutions for smartcard package manufacturers, chip makers and integrators in particular in the Payment, Telecommunications, eID and Transport & Access markets.

Since 2016, based on its unique know-how for the Chip-on-Flex technology Linxens becomes a light source manufacturer.

Linxens employs 3500+ employees worldwide, with manufacturing and R&D sites in Europe and Asia. Linxens achieved a turnover of 320 M€ in 2015. For more information, visit www.linxens.com

Why Join Linxens:

Linxens teams are made of highly-skilled professionals who wish to surpass themselves in a results-oriented culture dedicated to their clients.Our people are fully committed to contribute to their team’s and the Group’s growth and performance. In this demanding and friendly work environment, they develop both personally and professionally

Employees who join us can expect the following;

• A corporate culture of Openness, Collective Performance and Teamwork
• Comprehensive on-boarding and integration programs
• Exciting career development opportunities
• Meaningful and challenging work
• Extensive employee benefits

Our Core Values

Trust

Every day, we put our unique know-how and our passion for our craft in action to produce flexible etched circuits with best-in-class quality. Our biggest reward, the trust our customers put in us.

Openness

Openness means looking elsewhere, taking on new challenges, new practices, without prejudice. This enables us to stay alert, agile and connected. That is how we imagine new solutions, accessible to everybody, everywhere, everytime

Collective Performance
